name: loop-spec
description: Loop-driven development from a spec file (or a prose description) for Claude Code. 6 phases (SPEC/DISCUSS/PLAN/EXECUTE/VERIFY/ITERATE, where ITERATE is the convergence loop that judges the result against the original goal and rewinds until converged or budget-spent), single-tier operation (fixed gates/budgets, structural fast-path for trivially-scoped plans, iterate convergence budget 10), fixed per-role model map (opus authors/judges incl. code review; sonnet implements), 4 execution styles, EXECUTE concurrency ladder (subagent/loop-fleet/team/workflow by DAG width), bundled loop-runner skill (bounded autonomous loops with verifier integrity, spec→plan compiler, fleet supervisor), teams-optional operation (explicit and implicit agent-team harnesses), required graphify code graph (queried to ground SPEC/DISCUSS/PLAN) + probe-before-assert grounding gate (committed EVIDENCE.md ledger via lib/evidence.sh + deterministic lib/grounding-lint.sh blocks ungrounded external-system claims in design artifacts), ponytail-ported simplicity mode (laziness ladder, on by default), user-gate verification flow, Ralph remediation loop, dynamic-workflow fan-out acceleration, workspace (multi-repo) mode, greenfield mode (net-new application bootstrap), autonomous mode (question-free headless runs: every ask self-answers with the recommended option, recorded in an auditable decisions record), standalone assess, quality-loop, debug (bounded triage/repro/fix/verify loop), and intake (anything -> spec draft -> cycle: slack/jira/email/prompt converter) skills.
